preview_text:
The model favors England at 61%, with Panama's win probability at 13%. This represents a clear gap in quality, though the underdog retains a meaningful chance of avoiding defeat.

The forecast suggests competitive conditions. A draw sits at 26%, making it the second-most-likely outcome. The win-probability spread of roughly 48 percentage points indicates England are favored, but the relatively high draw probability reflects the possibility of a tightly contested match where neither side pulls decisively clear.

The sportsbook consensus moves notably in England's favor, rating them at 78% and Panama at just 8%—a 16-point gap from the model's England projection and a 5-point gap on Panama. The books also price draws lower at 15%. This divergence suggests sportsbooks view the match as less competitive than the model estimates. No Polymarket data is available yet.
